api_key.how_it_works_subtitle: Your API token is a JWT. Extending or revoking always issues a new JWT — you must copy it and update your integrations.
api_key.step1_desc: Your account has one API access token (JWT) shown on this page. Use it to authenticate server-side requests.
api_key.step2_desc: Include the token in every request: Authorization: Bearer <your_token>.
api_key.step3_desc: Extend Validity issues a new JWT with the expiration you select. Revoke invalidates every previous JWT and issues one new JWT.
api_key.renew_description: Issues a new JWT with the expiration period you select. Copy it and update your integrations — the existing JWT string is not extended in place.
api_key.extend_new_token_notice: This action generates a new JWT. After confirming, copy the new token and update every integration — your current token string will not be modified in place.
api_key.token_renewed_success: New token generated! Copy it and update your integrations.

overview.title: How API tokens work
overview.intro: Your API token is a JWT credential that authenticates your server-side requests to Verifik.
overview.point1: The token shown on this page is a signed JWT. It cannot be edited in place — extending or revoking always creates a new JWT string.
overview.point2: Every JWT has an expiration date. When it expires, API requests return authentication errors until you extend or revoke to get a new one.
overview.point3: Extend Validity issues a new JWT with the expiration period you choose. Copy the new token and update every integration that calls Verifik.
overview.point4: Revoke & Generate New also issues a new JWT, but immediately invalidates every previously issued token for your account.
overview.point5: Multiple servers or environments can each store the latest JWT. After extend or revoke, make sure they all use the new token string.

token.title: Using your access token
token.intro: Your token must be sent on every API request as a Bearer credential.
token.point1: Copy the token from this page and store it securely in your backend — never expose it in client-side apps, mobile apps, or public repositories.
token.point2: Add it to the Authorization header on every request to api.verifik.co (or your sandbox base URL).
token.point3: After extend or revoke, the token string on this page changes. Update every service still using the previous JWT.

extend.title: Extending token validity
extend.intro: Extend Validity does not modify your current JWT. It issues a brand-new JWT with the expiration period you select.
extend.point1: Choose how many months (1–36). The new JWT expires after that period, counted from the moment you extend — not from when the previous token was created.
extend.point2: You receive a new token string on this page immediately. Copy it and update every service, script, or environment variable that calls the Verifik API.
extend.point3: Unlike revoke, extend does not immediately invalidate previously issued JWTs — they keep working until their own expiration. Still, deploy the new token everywhere.
extend.point4: Extend before your current token expires to avoid downtime. If it already expired, extend or revoke to get a working JWT.
extend.note: You cannot push the expiration date forward on an existing JWT string. Extend always generates a new token.

revoke.title: Revoking and generating a new token
revoke.intro: This issues a new JWT and immediately invalidates every token previously issued for your account.
revoke.point1: Revoke invalidates all previous JWTs right away, including tokens created by earlier extend actions.
revoke.point2: You receive one new token string immediately after confirming. Copy it and update every integration that calls the API.
revoke.point3: Applications still using an old token will receive 401 errors until updated.
revoke.point4: Use revoke only when a token may have been leaked, shared insecurely, or you need to force a full credential rotation.
revoke.note: If you need a longer-lived credential without cutting off every active token immediately, use Extend Validity — but you still must deploy the new JWT string.
